This fax may contain sensitive and confidential personal health information that is being
sent for the sole use of the intended recipient. Unintended recipients are directed to
securely destroy any materials received. You are hereby notified that the unauthorized
disclosure or other unlawful use of this fax or any personal health information is
prohibited. To the extent patient information contained in this fax is subject to 42 CFR
Part 2, this regulation prohibits unauthorized disclosure of these records.
If you received this fax in error, please visit www.athenahealth.com/NotMyFax to notify
the sender and confirm that the information will be destroyed. If you do not have
internet access, please call 1-888-482-8436 to notify the sender and confirm that the
information will be destroyed. Thank you for your attention and cooperation.

JASON SHERMAN, MD: 18444 N. 25TH AVENUE, SUITE 310, PHOENIX, AZ 85023-1266, Ph. tel: (866) 974-2673
-gabapentin did not help her upper limb paresthesias; consider lyrica
-Will repeat a L5-S1 L ESI to target her L4-5 central stenosis. The procedure recommended, with its
inherent risks and realistic benefits, was discussed in detail including but not limited to death, pain,
bleeding, Infection, headache, nerve Injury (elther mlld and translent or severe such as spinal cord
injury with secondary paresis), reaction to medications including raised blood glucose levels, breathing
difficulties, or cardiac difficulties. The patient was counseled on conscious sedation in the event that
this is used during the procedure.
-Continue use of her SCS unit. She was using it at her previous residence, but her aid is not sure if she
has the remote at her new residence.
-MRI lumbar spine reviewed and scanned into her chart.
-She is not interested in consulting with a spine surgeon.
-She was seen by Dr. Sumner and did PT. She is not interested in surgery for this.

14520 W Granite Valley Dr, Sun City
14520 W Granite Valley Dr, Sun City
Opiate agreement/ Medication Management Agreement reviewed/signed in the office. Agreement has been jsherman56 Not
Discussed the risks of opioid medication for pain management. These risks include: abnormal thinking,
constipation, addiction, medication tolerance, nausea, physical dependence, opioid-induced hyperalgesia
(increase in pain from opioid medications), respiratory depression, sedation and substance abuse.
The patient has been warned to keep medication in a safe place as lost or stolen medication will not be
They have also been told not to drink alcohol or illicit drugs and to avoid taking other central nervous
system depressant such as benzodiazepine due to the synergistic effect which can substantially increase
The patient was specifically told not to drive or operate heavy machinery as this medication may adversely
The patient has been informed that The CORE Institute conforms to all the recommendations of the
Arizona Medical Board. The AZ State Board of Pharmacy prescription monitoring web site was reviewed
today and reveals that the patient is adherent and does not reveal any suspicious activity to suggest
Urine drug screens (UDS) will be periodically obtained to monitor for compliance, as per The CORE
Institute policy. UDS done on 3/7/23 was consistent for prescribed opiates and EtS. She states that she
only takes this as needed and if she does not use this when she uses alcohol. An ODS was done on
1/30/24, 12/30/24, 3/31/25 were consistent for her prescribed medications.
She is low risk for opiod abuse per ORT.
Physical Therapist Referral for Spinal stenosis of lumbar region
PT for back strengthening and endurance 2-3x/week for 4-6 weeks

CORE - NAO 8952 E Desert Cove Avenue, SCOTTSDALE AZ 85260-6740
Basic HPI Reported by patient. Chief complaint: pain; weakness
Body part(s) affected: hip - bilateral; thigh bilateral; knee - bilateral; lower leg/calf bilateral; ankle bilateral; lower back
Mechanism: no specific incident or unknown
Prior treatments: OTC medications - helped a little; physical therapy - did not help
Prior testing: x-rays; MRI; CT scan
Previous surgery for this problem: no
Emergency department or urgent care referral: no
Lumbar Spine Reported by patient. Hand dominance: right
Radiation: radiation to right leg ( hip
Pain level: lumbar spine 0/10; right leg /10 right hip - 0)
Modifying factors: Lwisting worsens; sitting worsens; standing worsens ( up straight ); walking - worsens; lying flat - improves: all ROM and al rest
Associated symptoms: no numbness; no tingling; no headache; no sleep interruption; Bilateral hands
Prior treatments: injection some improvement ( 8/6/25 L5-S1 interlaminar epidural steroid injection ): physical therapy some improvement
Ms. Haug returns to clinic for ongoing management of her back pain and hand paresthesias.
She does have some aching pain in the low back that only mildly radiates into the posterior legs. This is worse with walking and standing. Better with
She also notes numbness/tingling and weakness in both hands. She had an EMG/NCS that showed severe CTS. She was seen by Dr. Sumner who sent
her to PT which has not helped. She does not want to have surgery.
She is taking oxycodone 5mg tid prn. This allows her to function and do her ADLs.
IMAGING: MRI lumbar spine (11/9/21): Severe canal stenosis at L4-5 secondary to spondylosis and anterolisthesis, there is spondylosis at L5-S1
Notes: Ceneral Appearance: Ceneral appearance: healthy appearing and no acute distress. Mood and affect: normal affect. Neurological System: Right lower
extremity motor function (muscle group): hip flexors 5/5, quadriceps 5/5, anterior tibial 5/5, EHL 5/5, and gastrocnemius 5/5. Left lower extremity motor
function (muscle group): hip flexors 5/5, quadriceps 5/5, anterior tibial 5/5, EHL 5/5, and gastrocnemius 5/5. Lumbar Spine: Lumbar motion: pain with motion
